title: Intraperitoneal Insulin Tolerance Test Protocol
author: Henri Brunengraber
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.yefftbn

steps.instruction: Fast mice for overnight or for 6 hours. Remove mouse from cage and put into a clean cage with water and no food.
Label the mice using a marker on their tail to quickly tell them apart
Weigh each mouse and record weight. Calculate the amount of insulin needed for 0.75U to 1.0 U /kg body weight. Record the volume for each mouse.
Dilute the stock of insulin (100U/ml) to 0.1u/ml
Take a glucose reading and record it as time zero. Dip tail into clotisol to clot blood.
Inject each mouse 3’ apart. Repeat #4 at 15min, 30 min, 60 min after IP injection of insulin.
Return mice to cage with food.
